candidate_lesson.lesson_text: When the 30-indicator consensus reports green_matches=0 and hist_hr=0%, treat directional conviction as unvalidated regardless of how high conv% is — only pairs with green_matches>=3 and hist_hr>60% have earned execution weight.
candidate_lesson.mechanism: green_matches counts how many times the current live indicator configuration has resolved profitably in historical memory. Zero matches means the present bull/bear tally is a novel configuration with no realized-edge basis; a high conv% only measures agreement among indicators, not that agreement's historical payoff. AUDUSD (12/30 bull, conv=75%, green_matches=3, hist_hr=68%) is the only pair whose signal has institutional-flow precedent; AUDNZD's 100% conv with green_matches=0 is unanchored consensus that historically has not paid.
candidate_lesson.testable_rule: if consensus.green_matches < 3 or consensus.hist_hr < 0.60: veto_entry(pair)  # conv% alone is insufficient
candidate_lesson.expected_impact: Filters out 4 of 5 currently-signaling pairs (GBPUSD, EURUSD, USDJPY, AUDNZD), concentrating capital on the one validated setup (AUDUSD) — expected win-rate lift and Sharpe contribution by cutting zero-precedent trades that dilute avgR.
candidate_lesson.evidence_from_harvest: Live consensus shows AUDNZD dir=SHORT at conv=100% but green_matches=0/hist_hr=0%, while AUDUSD at conv=75% carries green_matches=3/hist_hr=68% — the two highest-conviction signals diverge entirely on historical realized edge, and 4 of 5 pairs show hist_hr=0%.

retirement_flag.reason: L8 sets USDJPY/AUDNZD VIX gate at 13, but live VIX is 16.73 and rising (15.67→16.73) — under L8 both pairs are already vetoed, so if AUDNZD keeps surfacing as a 100%-conv candidate the gate may be bypassed somewhere in the sizing path and needs an audit.

reflection.system_health_note: Zero trades in the last 7 days with regime=UNKNOWN and TradingView at 0 signals means the system is in a low-confidence CALIBRATING state — the only defensible action is tightening entry gates, not adding exposure.
